text.title: A11y No-Regression PR Gate — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules for an accessibility regression gate that blocks PRs without becoming a ceremony everyone routes around.

rule.statement: The gate fails ONLY on NEW violations vs the stored baseline. Pre-existing violations do not block PRs. A PR that introduces 1 new violation AND fixes 5 pre-existing ones is GREEN (net negative).

rationale.source: Continuous improvement / ratchet pattern, axe-linter docs

rule.rationale: Demanding zero violations on day one blocks every PR and creates a culture of waivers. Net-regression lets teams ship while ratcheting down debt. Empirically, teams with net-regression gates close 70-80% of a11y debt within 12 months; teams with zero-violation gates close ~10%.

rule.statement: The baseline JSON MUST be committed to the repo at a known path (e.g., `.a11y/baseline.json`) and updated only via reviewed PRs. The gate generates the baseline from main / trunk and refuses to mutate it from feature branches.
rule.rationale: Baseline drift through silent edits destroys the ratchet. VCS-tracked baseline makes every change reviewable. Refusing feature-branch baseline updates prevents "I just added my violations to the baseline" smuggling.

rule.statement: A waiver bypasses the gate only when the waiver file (`.a11y-waivers.json`) contains an entry with: WCAG criterion ID, route, element selector, rationale (sentence), reviewer name, expiry date (max 90 days). Waivers MUST be reviewed by an a11y-aware lead and expire automatically.

rationale.source: ITIL change management, GDPR data-processing register patterns

rule.rationale: Waivers without expiry become permanent debt. Forcing rationale + reviewer makes waivers a deliberate decision with an owner. The 90-day cap forces re-evaluation; chronic items get fixed or upgraded to "won't fix" with explicit business justification.

rule.statement: The gate scans only routes / Storybook stories impacted by the PR diff. A diff mapping (file -> routes) MUST exist in the repo (e.g., `.a11y/route-map.json`) OR be derivable from framework conventions (Next.js pages dir, Storybook story files). Full-site scans run on a nightly schedule, not on every PR.

rationale.source: CI/CD performance research, axe-core CI patterns

rule.rationale: Scanning every route on every PR is too slow (5-30 min) and creates flakes that erode trust. Scoped scans run in 30-90 sec and stay green when irrelevant. Nightly full scans catch cross-route regressions without blocking the merge queue.

rule.statement: The gate's block decision is severity-aware. New violations at axe-core severity "critical" or "serious" block automatically. "Moderate" and "minor" violations require explicit ack in the PR description ("a11y-ack: 2 moderate violations added — tracked in TICKET-123") but do not auto-block.
rule.rationale: Treating all violations equally creates noise. Critical / serious correlate with actual user impact (keyboard traps, missing labels, color contrast failures). Moderate / minor (e.g., heading order off, redundant landmarks) matter but rarely warrant PR-blocking on a single offense.

example.title: Gate output for a net-zero PR
example.code:
PR #1247 - "Refactor PricingCard component" Routes scanned: /pricing, /pricing/enterprise, story:PricingCard/Default
Baseline diff: - NEW critical: 0 - NEW serious: 0 - NEW moderate: 1 (heading-order in PricingCard footer) - NEW minor: 0 - FIXED: 3 (color-contrast on /pricing CTA, region label, missing input label)
Decision: PASS (no critical/serious added; moderate requires ack) PR description contains: "a11y-ack: 1 moderate heading-order regression accepted, ticket A11Y-145 created" Net: -2 violations against baseline. Baseline NOT auto-updated; fix-PR will rebase baseline after merge.
example.note: Illustrates r1, r4, r5: net-regression accounting, scoped scan, severity-aware block decision.

example.title: Waiver-everything pattern
example.code:
.a11y-waivers.json: 47 entries, all with rationale "addressed in next sprint", expiry dates set 5 years in the future, reviewer field empty.
PR #1340: introduces 6 new violations. Author adds 6 entries to waivers file, sets reviewer to themselves, gate goes green, merge proceeds.
Repair: enforce r3 strictly. Reviewer field MUST be a different person from the PR author. Expiry MUST be <= 90 days. Schema validator rejects entries that violate these. The waiver process is for exceptions, not for routing around the gate.
example.note: Illustrates r3 violation — waiver bypass that defeats the gate.
